name: OpenAI
description: Maker of GPT and the OpenAI API. Offers frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4), reasoning (o4-mini), and the open-source gpt-oss family.

name: Anthropic
description: Maker of the Claude model family. Flagship Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, plus Sonnet and Haiku tiers. Known for safety research.

name: Google
description: Maker of Gemini multimodal models with up to 2M context. Current flagship Gemini 3.5 Flash; also open-weights Gemma.

name: xAI
description: Maker of Grok models. Current flagship Grok 4.3 offers 1M context at $1.25/$2.50 per 1M tokens — strong value.

name: DeepSeek
description: Chinese AI lab offering extremely cheap APIs with aggressive prompt caching. V4 Flash at $0.14/$0.28 per 1M tokens.

name: Mistral
description: European AI lab. Mistral Large 3 at $0.50/$1.50 per 1M tokens, plus Codestral for code and open-weight models.

name: Amazon
description: Maker of the Nova model family on AWS. Nova Micro at $0.035/$0.14 per 1M tokens is among the cheapest hosted APIs.

name: Cohere
description: Enterprise-focused lab. Command A flagship at $1/$2 per 1M tokens — a major price drop vs. the older Command R+.

name: Meta
description: Maker of the open-weight Llama family. Llama 4 Scout (17B, 16 experts, 10M context) and Llama 3.3 70B are widely hosted.

name: Groq
description: Inference platform running open models on custom LPU chips for extreme speed. Up to 1000 tokens/sec, cheapest hosted prices for many open models.

name: Together
description: Inference platform hosting 200+ open-source models with simple per-token pricing. Strong coverage of Qwen, GLM, Kimi, MiniMax, and DeepSeek variants.

name: Fireworks
description: Inference platform serving open-weight models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, gpt-oss) on its own infrastructure at first-party per-token pricing — distinct rates worth comparing per host.

name: Perplexity
description: Search-augmented AI from Perplexity. The Sonar family (Sonar, Sonar Pro, Sonar Reasoning Pro, Sonar Deep Research) adds live web grounding, from $1/1M tokens.

name: NVIDIA
description: NVIDIA's Nemotron models for enterprise and agentic use — Llama Nemotron Ultra 253B and Nemotron 3 Ultra, from $0.60/$3.60 per 1M tokens.

name: IBM
description: IBM's open-weight Granite 4 family for enterprise workloads — Granite 4 H Large down to Granite 4.0 Micro at $0.017/1M input tokens.

name: AI21 Labs
description: Israeli AI lab behind the Jamba hybrid SSM-Transformer models — Jamba Large and Jamba Mini ($0.20/$0.40 per 1M) with a 256K-token context.

name: Reka
description: Multimodal AI lab. Reka Core, Flash, and Edge span frontier to on-device inference, with Reka Edge from $0.10/1M tokens.

name: Voyage AI
description: Voyage AI (a MongoDB company) builds high-accuracy embedding and reranking models for retrieval and RAG, billed per 1M input tokens with no output-token charge.

name: Alibaba
description: Alibaba's Qwen models — Qwen3-Max, Qwen-Plus, and Qwen-Turbo (from $0.05/$0.20 per 1M) with context windows up to 1M tokens.

name: Z.AI
description: Z.AI (formerly Zhipu AI) — makers of the GLM model family. Frontier and open-weight models with competitive pricing.

name: Moonshot
description: Chinese AI lab behind the Kimi models. Kimi K2.5 through K2.7 Code offer a 256K-token context from $0.60/1M input tokens.

name: MiniMax
description: Chinese AI lab. The MiniMax-M2 series (M2 through M2.7, plus M3 at 1M context) starts at $0.30/$1.20 per 1M tokens.

name: 01.AI
description: Founded by Kai-Fu Lee. Yi Large is a bilingual English/Chinese model at $3/$9 per 1M tokens with a 32K context window.

name: Baichuan
description: Chinese AI lab. Baichuan M2-32B is an open-weight 32B model priced at $0.07/1M for both input and output tokens.

name: Tencent
description: Chinese technology conglomerate. Its Hunyuan family is served via Tencent Cloud's TokenHub API; Hunyuan Hy3 is a 295B-parameter MoE agentic model listed in RMB (converted to USD per the FX policy).
